programs.name: Medicaid for Seniors/Disabled
programs.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescription medications, and long-term care to low-income seniors. It significantly reduces out-of-pocket medical expenses for eligible individuals.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled individuals who meet income and resource limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers medical services, long-term care, and reduces out-of-pocket costs.[3]

programs.name: Community Based Alternatives (CBA) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Pays for services associated with assisted living facilities and community-based alternatives to institutional care. It supports seniors in living independently rather than in nursing homes, though it does not cover room and board.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors needing long-term care services with functional or geographic restrictions.
programs.benefit_value: Covers personal care, nursing, and assisted living services (excludes room/board).[5]

programs.name: STAR+PLUS
programs.what_it_does: Offers comprehensive managed care support tailored to individual needs, including access to essential long-term services. It enhances quality of care for seniors through coordinated healthcare and support services.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors, particularly in certain regions, needing long-term care.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive long-term care services including nursing and personal care.[4][5]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings. It coordinates all needed care to help participants avoid nursing home placement.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing home level care but able to live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation covering medical, social, and long-term care services.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: QMB covers Medicare Parts A & B premiums, deductibles, and copayments; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums only. These programs help low-income seniors afford Medicare costs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income limits (e.g., QMB: $1,275 individual/$1,724 couple monthly).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Premiums, deductibles, copays (QMB); Part B premiums (SLMB/QI).[1]

programs.name: Texas Simplified Application Project (TSAP) for SNAP
programs.what_it_does: Provides food assistance through a simplified application process for seniors with longer certification periods and no renewal interviews. It helps eligible seniors purchase nutritious food.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors (60+) with income/resources under limits (e.g., $4,500 resource limit).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Average $188/month for older adults; EBT card for food purchases.[1][4]

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers emergency energy services and home weatherization to help with utility bills. It assists low-income households in maintaining affordable home energy.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with income below 150% federal poverty level, SSI/SNAP/Veterans benefits recipients.[1]
programs.benefit_value: One-time payments or crisis aid based on available federal funding.[1]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home weatherization services to improve energy efficiency and reduce utility costs. It targets low-income households including seniors.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (income <150% FPL) and benefit recipients.[1]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare options, including savings programs and Extra Help. It helps seniors navigate Medicare benefits and appeals.
programs.who_its_for: Texas seniors eligible for Medicare seeking benefits counseling.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized Medicare counseling and application assistance.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to combat food insecurity and promote health. It provides regular hot meals and sometimes friendly visits.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals independently.
programs.benefit_value: Daily/weekly home-delivered meals; often low/no cost for eligible.

programs.name: Community Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides in-home assistance, caregiver training, and respite care to support family caregivers. It promotes senior independence through community resources.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors aged 60+ or disabled adults 18+.
programs.benefit_value: Respite care, training, in-home aid; enhanced by ADRC grants.[4]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service jobs with training to unemployed low-income seniors. It provides work experience and wage subsidies leading to employment.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed seniors aged 55+ with low income (≤125% poverty level).
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/week) plus training.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. It helps seniors access rights and resolve disputes.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ facing legal barriers.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal advice, representation, and education.[1]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints and protecting rights. It investigates issues and promotes quality care.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities in Texas.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy, complaint resolution, and rights protection.
